description: Frozen benchmark-first proxy base that turns the current reviewer-facing benchmark stack into one end-to-end proxy baseline artifact.

verdict.note: The local benchmark-first proxy base is now complete and remains frozen as a support layer: adapter grounding, minimal external panel, broader reviewer section, benchmark-first primary surface, and exact non-proxy frontier closure are all still explicit, even though the main blocker has now moved up to a benchmark-native primary base.

reviewer_sequence: Start from the broader reviewer-facing benchmark section rather than the synthetic trio.
Treat the benchmark-first primary surface as the local proxy presentation layer that now feeds into the native primary base.
Use the exact non-proxy frontier closure as evidence that the current stress frontier is no longer held together by proxy rows.
remaining_gaps: Expand the broader benchmark reviewer section into more slice families and larger frozen coverage once the current proxy base stays stable.
Reduce how much reviewer-facing interpretation still depends on synthetic-reference support artifacts.
